mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and higher discount rates for future earnings compress equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has been in a tightening cycle since 2004
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust economic expansion, which translates to higher aggregate demand, corporate revenue growth, and positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts signals a contraction in a key cyclical sector of the economy, leading to negative wealth effects and reduced construction activity, which negatively impacts broader equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market has shown signs of peaking in late 2005/early 2006
failure_condition: When the year-over-year HOUST growth is below -15%, the S&P 500 fails to produce negative median 60-day returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and accelerating inflation forces the central bank to pursue aggressively hawkish policy. This joint condition creates a strong headwind for equities as the market anticipates restrictive policy to cool an overheating economy.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and 6-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 3.0%
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal does not remain consistently negative over a 3-year walk-forward test period.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing momentum in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing and business investment. This decline in output is a leading indicator of contracting corporate profits, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO shows a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surprise contraction in monthly retail sales signals an immediate weakening of consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This negative demand shock leads to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate's 3-month moving average from its 12-month low (a 'Sahm Rule' precursor) signals the start of a labor market deterioration. This is a powerful leading indicator of recession, prompting a risk-off sentiment in equity markets.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.3pp from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently at a cyclical low of 4.7%
failure_condition: Following a trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative on average with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When strong real GDP growth coexists with an aggressively tightening Federal Reserve, the market anticipates that restrictive monetary policy will eventually choke off the expansion. This conflict between strong current growth and future policy headwinds leads to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in GDPC1 > 3.0% and 6-month change in FEDFUNDS > 1.5%
failure_condition: The specified joint condition fails to predict negative median 60-day S&P 500 returns over two full business cycles.
